question: According to Lecture 1, which phrase is the business-focused definition of marketing?

answers.A: The set of strategies and activities by which companies acquire and engage customers, build strong customer relationships, and create superior customer value in order to capture value from customers in return.
answers.B: A short-term sales tactic focused only on price cuts.
answers.C: A production system for reducing costs and increasing efficiency.
answers.D: A distribution process for moving products to retailers.

question: According to Lecture 1, which is Goal 1 of marketing?

answers.A: Attract new customers by promising superior value
answers.B: Keep and grow current customers by delivering the promised value
answers.C: Help drive long-term company profitability by recapturing some of the delivered value from satisfied and loyal customers
answers.D: Set the lowest possible price in the market

question: According to Lecture 1, which is Goal 2 of marketing?

answers.A: Keep and grow current customers by delivering the promised value
answers.B: Attract new customers by promising superior value
answers.C: Help drive long-term company profitability by recapturing some of the delivered value from satisfied and loyal customers
answers.D: Reduce advertising to improve margins

question: According to Lecture 1, which is Goal 3 of marketing?

answers.A: Help drive long-term company profitability by recapturing some of the delivered value from satisfied and loyal customers
answers.B: Attract new customers by promising superior value
answers.C: Keep and grow current customers by delivering the promised value
answers.D: Match competitor prices in every category

question: In Lecture 1, which exact phrase defines a need?

answers.A: State of felt deprivation
answers.B: Needs shaped by culture & individual personality
answers.C: Human wants backed by buying power
answers.D: Easy, instant, mobile messaging

question: In Lecture 1, which exact phrase defines a want?

answers.A: Needs shaped by culture & individual personality
answers.B: State of felt deprivation
answers.C: Human wants backed by buying power
answers.D: Connect with others

question: In Lecture 1, which exact phrase defines demand?

answers.A: Human wants backed by buying power
answers.B: State of felt deprivation
answers.C: Needs shaped by culture & individual personality
answers.D: Connect with others

question: In Lecture 1, which example is listed for a want?

answers.A: Easy, instant, mobile messaging
answers.B: Connect with others
answers.C: Free messaging application, adopted globally
answers.D: Creating and exchanging value with others

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact phrase defines the marketing environment?

answers.A: The marketing environment includes the actors and forces outside marketing that affect marketing management’s ability to build and maintain successful relationships with target customers.
answers.B: Consumer buyer behavior is how people decide what to buy, use, and recommend.
answers.C: Customer insights is a deep understanding of needs, pain points, and behavior.
answers.D: Marketing channel: A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product/service available for use or consumption.

question: Which exact Lecture 2 phrase completes the slide note under the marketing environment?

answers.A: Everything outside of your product that can help or hurt demand
answers.B: The end-to-end customer journey
answers.C: The full mix of benefits upon which a brand is positioned
answers.D: The desired customer service level at the lowest total cost

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact item appears under microenvironmental actors?

answers.A: Marketing Intermediaries (how you reach customers): app stores, resellers, agencies, marketplaces
answers.B: Demographic: who your users are (age, income, location…)
answers.C: Technological: platform shifts, AI, cybersecurity threats, new standards
answers.D: Natural/Environmental: climate risk, energy costs, sustainability

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact item appears under microenvironmental actors?

answers.A: Public (groups that shape reputation): media, online communities, regulators, investors
answers.B: Economic: budgets, inflation, interest rates, hiring freezes
answers.C: Cultural/Social: values, trust, ethics, privacy attitudes
answers.D: Demographic: who your users are (age, income, location…)

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact item appears under macroenvironmental actors?

answers.A: Economic: budgets, inflation, interest rates, hiring freezes
answers.B: Customers (buyers & users): needs, budgets, switching costs, decision-makers
answers.C: Marketing Intermediaries (how you reach customers): app stores, resellers, agencies, marketplaces
answers.D: Competitors (and substitutes): direct rivals + 'do nothing' or spreadsheets

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact item appears under macroenvironmental actors?

answers.A: Political/Legal: privacy, IP, accessibility, competition laws
answers.B: Company (internal teams): product, engineering sales, support
answers.C: Customers (buyers & users): needs, budgets, switching costs, decision-makers
answers.D: Public (groups that shape reputation): media, online communities, regulators, investors

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact phrase appears under why consumer behavior matters?

answers.A: To understand how consumers make decisions
answers.B: To coordinate communication tools to deliver a clear, consistent, and compelling brand message.
answers.C: To deliver the desired customer service level at the lowest total cost.
answers.D: To add offerings at a new price/market level.

question: According to Lecture 2, which exact phrase appears under consumer behavior in tech?

answers.A: Advocacy: What makes people leave reviews, share, and recommend?
answers.B: Awareness: introduce new products or build primary demand
answers.C: Trialability: sandbox demo and a 7-day free trial
answers.D: Exporting: selling products produced in one country to buyers in another

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact sentence appears on the customer insights slide?

answers.A: Data tells you what happened. Insight tells you why. Strategy tells you what to do.
answers.B: Price is the money charged for a product/service.
answers.C: Advertising is paid, non-personal promotion of ideas, goods, or services.
answers.D: One brand should feel like one voice, no matter where the customer encounters it.

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact phrase defines customer insights?

answers.A: Customer Insights is a deep understanding of needs, pain points, and behavior
answers.B: Customer-perceived value refers to benefits relative to costs versus competing offers
answers.C: A market segment is a group of buyers with similar needs/characteristics
answers.D: Product: anything offered to satisfy a need or want

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact phrase appears under big data?

answers.A: Big data is high-volume, high-velocity, high-variety data from digital systems
answers.B: Big data is any information previously collected for another purpose
answers.C: Big data is the specific blend of promotion tools the company uses
answers.D: Big data is a short-term incentive to encourage the purchase or sale of a product or service

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact item is listed as a source under big data?

answers.A: product usage analytics, transactions, CRM, support tickets, social reviews
answers.B: TV, digital, radio etc.
answers.C: discounts, coupons, displays, demonstrations, events
answers.D: mobile-first and short-form video discovery

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact sentence appears on the managing marketing info slide?

answers.A: Insight without action is trivia.
answers.B: Sell value, not price.
answers.C: Good logos are simple, readable and scalable.
answers.D: One brand should feel like one voice.

question: According to Lecture 3, which three sources are listed under developing marketing information?

answers.A: Internal Data – what customers do; Marketing Intelligence – what’s happening outside the company; Marketing Research – what customers say + why they do it
answers.B: Advertising – remind customers; PR – engage publics; digital – go global
answers.C: Need – want – demand
answers.D: Product – price – place

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact phrase defines primary data?

answers.A: information collected for the specific purpose at-hand.
answers.B: information previously collected for another purpose that can be used.
answers.C: data you already collect from running the business and product
answers.D: the actors and forces outside marketing

question: According to Lecture 3, which exact phrase defines secondary data?

answers.A: information previously collected for another purpose that can be used.
answers.B: information collected for the specific purpose at-hand.
answers.C: the process: Collect → clean → analyze → test → share → act
answers.D: a deep understanding of needs, pain points, and behavior

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase defines a product?

answers.A: Product: anything offered to satisfy a need or want
answers.B: Price is the money charged for a product/service
answers.C: Advertising is paid, non-personal promotion of ideas, goods, or services
answers.D: Marketing channel: A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product/service available for use or consumption.

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase appears directly under the product definition?

answers.A: Includes goods, services, experiences, people, places, ideas
answers.B: Includes wholesalers, retailers, distributors, and logistics providers
answers.C: Includes budgets, inflation, interest rates, hiring freezes
answers.D: Includes websites, social media, email, and mobile

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase defines services?

answers.A: Services are intangible benefits (you use them – you don’t own them)
answers.B: Services are products sold only through digital channels
answers.C: Services are short-term incentives to encourage purchase
answers.D: Services are the same as physical goods but cheaper

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase appears on products, services, and experiences?

answers.A: Experience is the differentiator (how it feels to buy / use / support)
answers.B: Price is the only 'P' that produces revenue
answers.C: Competition laws shape markets and buyer behavior
answers.D: Informative advertising introduces new products or builds primary demand

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase labels the first product level?

answers.A: Core Value: What problem are we solving?
answers.B: Actual Product: What is it, exactly?
answers.C: Augmented Product: What makes it better to choose / use?
answers.D: Product Mix Decisions = the full portfolio

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase describes convenience products?

answers.A: Convenience: frequent, low effort
answers.B: Shopping: compare options
answers.C: Specialty: strong brand preference; high involvement
answers.D: Unsought: not actively looking; need awareness

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact sentence appears under B2B product classifications?

answers.A: B2B buyers care most about ROI, reliability, support & risk reduction
answers.B: B2C buyers care most about prestige, celebrity, and trend cycles
answers.C: Consumers only buy through direct channels
answers.D: Intermediaries exist to eliminate all costs

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact line appears under product attributes?

answers.A: Good attributes reduce friction → higher adoption & retention
answers.B: Good attributes reduce prices → higher margins only
answers.C: Good attributes eliminate the need for promotion
answers.D: Good attributes guarantee market leadership

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase distinguishes performance quality?

answers.A: Performance Quality: how well it works (speed, accuracy, features work)
answers.B: Conformance Quality: how consistently it works (bugs, uptime, reliability)
answers.C: Style: how it looks and feels (visual appeal)
answers.D: Design: how it works (usability + usefulness)

question: According to Lecture 4, which exact phrase defines design?

answers.A: Design: how it works (usability + usefulness)
answers.B: Style: how it looks and feels (visual appeal)
answers.C: Quality: reliability, performance, security
answers.D: Features: capabilities that solve the user’s job

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase defines price?

answers.A: Price is the money charged for a product/service – or more broadly, the total value customers exchange to receive the benefits (money + time + effort + risk)
answers.B: Price is the standard markup added to cost
answers.C: Price is whatever competitors charge
answers.D: Price is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact sentence appears under important notes?

answers.A: Price is the only “P’ that produces revenue; the others (product, place, promotion) create costs.
answers.B: Place is the only 'P' that produces revenue; the others create costs.
answers.C: Promotion is the only 'P' that produces revenue; the others create costs.
answers.D: Product is the only 'P' that produces revenue; the others create costs.

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under customer-value based pricing?

answers.A: Set price based on the customer’s perceived value (benefits), not the sellers cost
answers.B: Set price based on the costs of producing, distributing, and selling
answers.C: Definition: Price = cost + standard markup
answers.D: Set price to cover costs (breakeven) or earn a target profit

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under customer-value based pricing?

answers.A: Customer-driven: start with what buyers value and will pay for
answers.B: Competition-driven: start with what rivals charge and copy it
answers.C: Supplier-driven: start with input cost increases
answers.D: Promotion-driven: start with the media budget

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under cost-based pricing?

answers.A: Set price based on the costs of producing, distributing, and selling
answers.B: Set price based on the customer’s perceived value (benefits), not the sellers cost
answers.C: Set price to introduce new products or build primary demand
answers.D: Set price to create a seamless customer experience across touchpoints

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase defines cost-plus pricing?

answers.A: Definition: Price = cost + standard markup
answers.B: Definition: Price = perceived value + standard markdown
answers.C: Definition: Price = competitor price + ad spend
answers.D: Definition: Price = fixed cost + variable cost only

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under cost-plus pricing?

answers.A: Why firms use it: simple, predictable, feels “fair”
answers.B: Why firms use it: coordinated and consistent across channels
answers.C: Why firms use it: creates one voice across all media
answers.D: Why firms use it: reduces mixed or confusing messages

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under new product pricing?

answers.A: Market-Skimming
answers.B: Reminder advertising
answers.C: Selective distribution
answers.D: Trade promotion

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under new product pricing?

answers.A: Market Penetration
answers.B: Branded entertainment
answers.C: Straight Product Extension
answers.D: Interactive Marketing

question: According to Lecture 5, which exact phrase appears under deceptive pricing?

answers.A: fake “regular” prices, drip fees, confusing price representation
answers.B: same marketing strategy across countries
answers.C: one clear and consistent brand message
answers.D: good-value pricing: the right mix of quality and service at a fair price

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines a marketing channel?

answers.A: Marketing channel (distribution channel): A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product/service available for use or consumption.
answers.B: The promotion mix is the specific blend of promotion tools that the company uses to persuasively communicate customer value and build customer relationships.
answers.C: Retailing includes all the activities in selling products or services directly to final consumers for their personal, nonbusiness use.
answers.D: Advertising is paid, non-personal promotion of ideas, goods, or services.

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase appears under why channels add value?

answers.A: Match supply with what customers want (right assortment)
answers.B: Build stronger brand awareness
answers.C: Keep the brand in customers’ minds and maintain relationships
answers.D: Introduce new products or build primary demand

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase appears under why channels add value?

answers.A: Bridge gaps: time, place, and possession
answers.B: Bridge gaps: quality, service, and price
answers.C: Bridge gaps: promotion, segmentation, and positioning
answers.D: Bridge gaps: awareness, interest, and desire

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines channel level?

answers.A: Channel Level: A layer of intermediaries that moves the product and ownership closer to the final buyer.
answers.B: A sales promotion aimed at organizational buyers
answers.C: A group of closely related products sold to similar customers
answers.D: A specific communication task with a defined target audience and time period

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines a direct channel?

answers.A: Direct channel: no intermediaries
answers.B: Indirect channel: one or more intermediaries
answers.C: Inbound logistics (into the company)
answers.D: Retailers are businesses whose sales come primarily from retailing.

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact item is listed as a key channel flow?

answers.A: Ownership flow
answers.B: Brand equity flow
answers.C: Culture flow
answers.D: Experience flow

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase appears under types of VMS?

answers.A: Corporate VMS
answers.B: Reminder VMS
answers.C: Persuasive VMS
answers.D: Experiential VMS

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase appears under types of VMS?

answers.A: Contractual VMS
answers.B: Integrated VMS
answers.C: Geographic VMS
answers.D: Lifestyle VMS

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines marketing logistics?

answers.A: Marketing logistics (physical distribution): planning and managing the flow of goods + information from origin to consumption. Goal: deliver the desired customer service level at the lowest total cost.
answers.B: Marketing logistics means maximizing delivery speed regardless of cost.
answers.C: Marketing logistics means eliminating all intermediaries.
answers.D: Marketing logistics means standardizing ads across countries.

question: According to Lecture 8, which exact phrase defines retailing?

answers.A: Retailing includes all the activities in selling products or services directly to final consumers for their personal, nonbusiness use.
answers.B: Wholesaling includes all activities involved in selling goods and services to those buying for resale or business use.
answers.C: Promotion is directed at final consumers to create demand.
answers.D: Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion by an identified sponsor.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines the promotion mix?

answers.A: The promotion mix is the specific blend of promotion tools that the company uses to persuasively communicate customer value and build customer relationships.
answers.B: Marketing channel (distribution channel): A set of interdependent organizations that help make a product/service available for use or consumption.
answers.C: Price is the money charged for a product/service.
answers.D: Customer insights is a deep understanding of needs, pain points, and behavior.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines advertising?

answers.A: Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion of ideas, goods, or services by an identified sponsor.
answers.B: Sales promotion is a short-term incentive to encourage the purchase or sale of a product or service.
answers.C: Personal selling is the personal interaction by the firm’s sales force for the purpose of engaging customers, making sales, and building customer relationships.
answers.D: Public relations involves building good relations with the company’s various publics by obtaining favorable publicity, building up a good corporate image, and handling or heading off unfavorable rumors, stories, and events.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines sales promotion?

answers.A: Sales promotion is a short-term incentive to encourage the purchase or sale of a product or service.
answers.B: Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion of ideas, goods, or services by an identified sponsor.
answers.C: Reminder advertising - keep the brand in customers’ minds and maintain relationships
answers.D: Exporting – selling products produced in one country to buyers in another

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines personal selling?

answers.A: Personal selling is the personal interaction by the firm’s sales force for the purpose of engaging customers, making sales, and building customer relationships.
answers.B: Public relations involves building good relations with the company’s various publics.
answers.C: Advertising is any paid form of nonpersonal presentation and promotion.
answers.D: Sales promotion is a short-term incentive.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase defines IMC?

answers.A: IMC = coordinating communication tools to deliver a clear, consistent, and compelling brand message.
answers.B: IMC = increasing ad frequency until sales rise.
answers.C: IMC = choosing the lowest-cost media mix.
answers.D: IMC = selling the same product everywhere.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact sentence appears under why it matters?

answers.A: One brand should feel like one voice, no matter where the customer encounters it.
answers.B: One price should feel like one deal, no matter where the customer shops.
answers.C: One product should feel like one feature set, no matter the segment.
answers.D: One channel should feel like one retailer, no matter the market.

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact step appears first in developing effective marketing communication?

answers.A: Identify the target audience
answers.B: Set communication objectives
answers.C: Design the message
answers.D: Choose communication channels

question: According to Lecture 9, which exact phrase appears under push and pull?

answers.A: Promotion is directed at final consumers to create demand.
answers.B: Promotion is directed at channel members to move products through the channel.
answers.C: Promotion is directed at suppliers to lower input costs.
answers.D: Promotion is directed at regulators to shape policy.

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase defines advertising?

answers.A: Paid, non-personal promotion of ideas, goods, or services
answers.B: A short-term incentive to encourage the purchase or sale of a product or service
answers.C: The personal interaction by the firm’s sales force
answers.D: Planning and managing the flow of goods + information from origin to consumption

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase appears as a purpose of advertising?

answers.A: Inform customers
answers.B: Break bulk into smaller lots
answers.C: Deliver the desired customer service level at the lowest total cost
answers.D: Collect → clean → analyze → test → share → act

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase appears under key advertising decisions?

answers.A: Set advertising objectives (inform, engage, persuade, remind)
answers.B: Set channel objectives (cost, control, adaptability)
answers.C: Set product objectives (quality, features, design)
answers.D: Set logistics objectives (inbound, outbound, reverse)

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase defines an advertising objective?

answers.A: A specific communication task with a defined target audience and time period
answers.B: A specific pricing task with a defined target margin and time period
answers.C: A specific logistics task with a defined service level and time period
answers.D: A specific segmentation task with a defined profile and time period

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase defines informative advertising?

answers.A: Informative advertising – introduce new products or build primary demand
answers.B: Persuasive advertising – build brand preference and encourage switching
answers.C: Reminder advertising - keep the brand in customers’ minds and maintain relationships
answers.D: Press relations – share newsworthy information with media outlets

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase appears under advertising budget factors?

answers.A: Stage in the product life cycle
answers.B: Line filling
answers.C: Buyer readiness stage
answers.D: Performance quality

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase appears under digital marketing process?

answers.A: Use digital channels (web, social, mobile)
answers.B: Use only physical retail outlets
answers.C: Use only TV, radio, and print
answers.D: Use only one channel to avoid mixed messages

question: According to Lecture 10, which exact phrase defines straight product extension?

answers.A: Straight Product Extension – sell the same product everywhere
answers.B: Product Adaptation – modify the product for local preferences
answers.C: Product Invention – create a new product for a specific market
answers.D: Adapted Marketing – adjust marketing for each country
